cycle1_influence.H1.2-E_surface_layer: Connected to network modifier theory (H2.2) and fractal evolution (H2.6)
cycle1_influence.H1.6-E_dual_SI: Extended to buffering mechanisms (H2.3) and long-term evolution (H2.4)

hypotheses.title: Activation Volume Scaling Laws Predict ASD-Drug Mechanical Stability Under Stress
hypotheses.bridge: Geochemical mineral activation volume → pressure-dependent reaction rates → ASD mechanical failure prediction
hypotheses.key_prediction: Optimal compression pressure P_opt = 20-40 MPa maximizes ASD stability
hypotheses.cycle1_connection: Builds on H1.1-E TST framework, adds pressure dependency

hypotheses.title: Silicate Network Modifier Analogies Predict Drug Loading Limits via Glass Transition Depression
hypotheses.bridge: Geochemical network modifier content → glass transition temperature → ASD drug loading optimization

hypotheses.title: Ionic Strength Buffering via Counterion Release Predicts pH-Independent ASD Dissolution
hypotheses.bridge: Geochemical weathering buffer systems → ion exchange equilibria → ASD dissolution pH stability
hypotheses.source_field: Natural pH buffering by mineral weathering
hypotheses.target_application: pH-robust ASD formulations for fed/fasted conditions
hypotheses.key_prediction: ASDs with high ionizable group density show pH-independent dissolution (pH 4-7)
hypotheses.cycle1_connection: Extends H1.6-E multi-component framework to pH buffering

hypotheses.title: Ostwald Ripening Competition Between LLPS and Crystallization Predicts Long-Term ASD Stability
hypotheses.bridge: Geochemical Ostwald ripening → competitive particle growth → ASD phase evolution prediction
hypotheses.target_application: Long-term ASD shelf-life prediction from short-term measurements
hypotheses.key_prediction: LLPS-stabilized ASDs maintain >80% supersaturation for months
hypotheses.cycle1_connection: Extends H1.6-E dual-SI framework with kinetic evolution

hypotheses.title: Congruent vs. Incongruent Dissolution Maps from Mineral Stoichiometry Predict ASD Release Mechanisms
hypotheses.bridge: Geochemical congruent/incongruent dissolution → stoichiometric release ratios → ASD drug:polymer release prediction
hypotheses.target_application: Rational design of controlled-release ASD formulations
hypotheses.key_prediction: Stoichiometric release ratio SR correlates with component solubility ratios
hypotheses.cycle1_connection: Connects to H1.2-E congruent/incongruent dissolution observations

hypotheses.title: Reactive Surface Area Evolution from Fractal Scaling Laws Predicts ASD Dissolution Rate Deceleration
hypotheses.bridge: Geochemical fractal surface roughening → reactive surface area evolution → ASD dissolution kinetics
hypotheses.source_field: Fractal scaling laws in mineral dissolution
hypotheses.key_prediction: Two-stage surface evolution: roughening → polymer masking
hypotheses.cycle1_connection: Builds on H1.1-E surface kinetics + H1.2-E surface evolution

quality_notes.diversity: Spans mechanical (H2.1), thermodynamic (H2.2, H2.3), kinetic (H2.4, H2.6), and analytical (H2.5) approaches
quality_notes.evolution_from_c1: All hypotheses build on or extend cycle 1 evolved insights while exploring new territories
quality_notes.testability: All hypotheses provide concrete experimental protocols with effort estimates
quality_notes.impact_potential: 4/6 hypotheses rated High or Transformative impact
